paper_overview.citation: Jaiswal, M., Hussain, J., Gupta, S. K., Nasr, M., & Nema, A. K. (2019). Comprehensive evaluation of water quality status for entire stretch of Yamuna River, India. Environmental Monitoring and Assessment, 191(4), 208. https://doi.org/10.1007/s10661-019-7312-8
paper_overview.study_region: entire Yamuna River stretch in India, including Uttarakhand, Delhi, and other states, joining the Ganges River at Allahabad.

data_sources.description: Supplementary file containing Table S1 (site coordinates), Table S2 and S3 (water quality parameters), Table S4 (Indian Standards classes), and Table S5 (WQI classifications).
Water quality parameters, ranges, and averages reported in the text and Table 1.

sites.description: Located in the upper zone of the Yamuna River. Characterized by low TDS and organic matter, suitable for drinking, irrigation, and aquatic life during monsoon. Exhibited low total alkalinity (142 mg/L) and total hardness (140 mg/L) during non-monsoon.
sites.source_quote: Group M-1, consisting of Paonta, Kalanaur, Mawi, and Palla, was distributed on the negative side of PC1. This group having low TDS and organic matter was suitable for drinking, irrigation, and aquatic life (IS 1982). Group NM-1 consisted of Paonta, Kalanaur, Mawi, and Palla. These sites are located at the upstream of the Yamuna River and described by low TA and TH values of 142 and 140 mg/L, respectively.

sites.description: Located in the midstream region of the Yamuna River. Characterized by high BOD5 (11.9 mg/L), NH4+ (2.7 mg/L), and NO3 (14.2 mg/L), but low DO (4.9 mg/L) during monsoon, indicating organic load from urban drains. During non-monsoon, showed high BOD5 (45.1 mg/L), NH4+ (3.1 mg/L), and NO3 (14.4 mg/L).
sites.source_quote: Group M-2 had the locations of Delhi, Mohana, and Mathura, which were positioned on the upper part of PC2. These sites had high BOD5 of 11.9 mg/L, NH4+ of 2.7 mg/L, and NO3 of 14.2 mg/L, but low DO of 4.9 mg/L, suggesting the contribution of an organic load from drains at the urban areas. Group NM-2 had the sites of Delhi, Mohana, and Mathura and composed of high BOD5, NH4+, and NO3 levels of 45.1, 3.1, and 14.4 mg/L, respectively.

sites.description: Located in the midstream region of the Yamuna River. Characterized by high dissolved salts (average TDS of 348 mg/L) during monsoon. During non-monsoon, showed high TDS (907 mg/L), TA (294 mg/L), TH (291 mg/L), EC (1512 μS/cm), Cl (209 mg/L), and SO42- (100 mg/L), potentially due to contamination from pesticide residues and drains.
sites.source_quote: Group M-3, containing Etawah and Agra, was located at the right PC1 direction. This group was characterized by high dissolved salts expressed by an average TDS of 348 mg/L. Group NM-3 contained Agra and Etawah. This group is located at the midstream of the Yamuna River, and it was characterized by high TDS (907 mg/L), TA (294 mg/L), TH (291 mg/L), and EC (1512 μS/cm). The high Cl (209 mg/L) and SO42- (100 mg/L) elements could be due to contamination with undetected and untreated pesticide residues that impose a toxic impact across the river.

sites.source_quote: Group M-3, containing Etawah and Agra, was located at the right PC1 direction. This group was characterized by high dissolved salts expressed by an average TDS of 348 mg/L. Group NM-3 contained Agra and Etawah. This group is located at the midstream of the Yamuna River, and it was characterized by high TDS (907 mg/L), TA (294 mg/L), TH (291 mg/L), EC (1512 μS/cm). The high Cl (209 mg/L) and SO42- (100 mg/L) elements could be due to contamination with undetected and untreated pesticide residues that impose a toxic impact across the river.

sites.description: Located in the lower zone of the Yamuna River. Characterized by high DO (average 7.3 mg/L) during monsoon. During non-monsoon, showed low BOD5 (1.78 mg/L), NH4+ (0.4 mg/L), NO3 (1.2 mg/L), and PO43 (0.2 mg/L).
sites.source_quote: Group M-4 of Auraiya, Hamirpur, and Pratappur was located at the negative PC2. These sites contained high DO concentration with an average value of 7.3 mg/L. Group NM-4 comprised Auraiya, Hamirpur, and Pratappur. These sites are located downstream of the Yamuna River, and they had low BOD5 (1.78 mg/L), NH4+ (0.4 mg/L), NO3¯ (1.2 mg/L), and PO43 (0.2 mg/L).

temporal_coverage.sampling_dates: monsoon (July to October) and non-monsoon (November to June)

extraction_notes: Latitude and longitude for sites are reported in a supplementary file (Table S1) which was not accessible. Detailed water quality parameter measurements (Tables S2 and S3) are reported in a supplementary file which was not accessible. Ranges and averages from the main text were used for descriptions where available. The unit for Electrical Conductivity (EC) was reported as "μ/cm" in Table 1, but "μS/cm" in the text (page 7). "μS/cm" was used as the standard unit. CAS numbers for parameters were not provided in the paper.
